summary: Validated dependency/build pipeline fix for React 19 + lucide-react-native. npm ci, TypeScript, lint, build:web and workflow/config checks passed; added regression tests for lockfile peers, Node 22, Vercel commands, and Camera icon import migration.

ui_bugs.issue: Browser automation could not connect to http://127.0.0.1:3000 (ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED), so runtime UI interaction validation did not execute.

integration_issues.issue: Instruction referenced frontend/.env REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL, but this Expo workspace has no /app/frontend/.env and no provided public app URL variable to drive browser test against user-facing endpoint.

action_items: For full UI runtime retest, provide the actual public app URL (or ensure frontend service is reachable in this environment) and rerun browser flows.
Optional hygiene: remove stale note in CUTSYNC_AUDIT.md that still mentions legacy-peer-deps workaround, to avoid confusion after fix.
critical_code_review_comments: package-lock.json now pins lucide-react-native@1.25.0 with peer react '^16.5.1 || ^17.0.0 || ^18.0.0 || ^19.0.0', matching React 19.2.3 and removing previous resolver conflict.
vercel.json is correctly configured with installCommand 'npm ci', buildCommand 'npm run build:web', outputDirectory 'dist'.
GitHub workflow install-and-build.yml is valid and runs setup-node(22), npm ci, lint, and build:web in sequence.
No legacy-peer-deps/force workaround present in release-critical files checked.
Icon import migration is consistent in referenced files (Camera used; removed Instagram import from lucide-react-native).

success_rate.frontend: 85% (typecheck/lint/build/config checks passed; runtime smoke blocked by connection refusal)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Core requested fix validations are green. Remaining gap is only runtime browser validation against a reachable public URL/service.
rca of the issue: Original failure was dependency resolution conflict: old lucide-react-native peer range excluded React 19. Fix worked by upgrading lucide-react-native to 1.25.0 and aligning lockfile; clean install now succeeds without legacy-peer-deps/force.
